title: Mortgage Application Velocity and Document Fraud Indicators
dataSources: `index=lending` loan application facts with `sourcetype=los:application`. **Sources:** major LOS platforms (**Ellie Mae Encompass**, **Calyx Point**, **Byte Software BytePro**) via **Splunk DB Connect** JDBC to originating SQL schemas **or** vendor **REST → HEC** micro-batching when APIs are authoritative. **Key fields:** `applicant_id`, `ssn_hash` (**never raw SSN**), normalized `property_address`, `stated_income`, `loan_amount`, `application_date`, `loan_officer_id`. **Volume:** ~500–10K applications/month depending on retail vs wholesale mix—adjust `stats` parallelism and alerting cadence accordingly.
app: **Splunk DB Connect** ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) **3.x+** installed on a **Heavy Forwarder** executing JDBC against the LOS database replica (`$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/splunk_app_db_connect/connections/`). **Encompass / ICE** pattern: query reporting tables or **Ellie Mae Data Connect** views with read-only service account; **BytePro / Calyx:** map loan file header + borrower + subject property tables. Push configuration via **Deployment Server**; store secrets in **password storage** or external vault integration. Alternative: vendor **webhook/HEC** token on Search Head if DB access is prohibited—normalize identical field names. Install **Splunk Enterprise Security** optional fraud correlation only if already licensed; not required for this SPL.
spl:
index=lending sourcetype=los:application
| bin _time span=30d
| stats count as apps, dc(property_address) as properties, dc(ssn_hash) as identities, values(stated_income) as incomes by applicant_id, _time
| where apps > 3 OR properties > 2
| eval income_variance=if(mvcount(incomes)>1, max(incomes)-min(incomes), 0)
| where income_variance > 20000 OR apps > 3
| sort -apps
description: Buckets each applicant's borrowing requests into thirty-day windows and contrasts application counts, distinct property fingerprints, hashed identity multiplicity, and income statements to reveal straw-buying or ring-style submission bursts before underwriting closes.
value: Credit risk and fraud operations slow predatory flipping schemes earlier in the funnel, safeguarding capital reserves while keeping honest borrowers from shoulder-surfing delays tied to manual reviews that could have been targeted.
implementation: Hash personally identifiable information consistently; align property addresses with geocoding keys; exclude legitimate portfolio investors via allow-list tags; pair hits with appraisal vendor scores and employer verification states before auto-decline chatter.
visualization: **Row 1:** Bubble chart: x=`apps`, y=`properties`, bubble size=`income_variance`, color=`loan_officer_id` (top N only for readability). **Row 2:** Choropleth or **cluster map** of `property_address` hotspots (only if geocoding approved). **Row 3:** Investigative table — `applicant_id`, `apps`, `properties`, `income_variance`, `loan_officer_id`, `loan_amount` band, risk tier from optional model lookup. **Row 4:** Executive strip — single values for **flagged apps / 1k applications** and **median time-to-review** from case system join.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: payloads originate from bespoke loan-origination APIs rather than a shared enterprise security acceleration package.

references.title: Splunk Lantern — Use Case Explorer: Financial Services
FinCEN — Advisories, Bulletins & Fact Sheets (mortgage fraud / SAR narratives)
FFIEC BSA/AML Examination Manual — Real Estate Loan Fraud contexts
CFPB — Mortgage data rules & HMDA context (fair lending awareness)

knownFalsePositives:
(1) **Relocation assistance and bridge-loan programs** — Employers may sponsor rapid multi-property applications across states with legitimately shifting `stated_income` (cost-of-living adjustments, dual-income onboarding). Mitigation: tag **`relocation_partner`** codes in LOS; suppress when verified against HR feed; escalate only if appraisals indicate flips inconsistent with timelines.

(2) **Professional property developers / builders** — Entities acquire several lots simultaneously while income appears volatile because draws hit different fiscal periods. Mitigation: **`investor_developer_allowlist`** keyed on `tax_id_hash`, require **commercial channel** tagging, bifurcate retail vs CRE pipelines for thresholds.

(3) **Refinance churn in falling-rate environments** — Borrowers rightly submit multiple competing applications within weeks; velocity spikes look like rings. Mitigation: join **rate_lock** ids, ignore duplicate `loan_amount` ±2% within 14 days, tune `apps > 3` upward during Fed-cut months with risk sign-off.

(4) **Divorce-related property transfers** — Rapid buyout + new purchase sequences create address churn and disjoint income affidavits. Mitigation: NLP or manual tag **`marital_event`** from loan notes (where legal), route to specialty underwriter queue instead of fraud decline.

(5) **Legitimate employment changes / commission jumps** — New W-2 or large commission checks cause `income_variance` across applications even when documentation is solid. Mitigation: pull **VOE refresh** timestamps; threshold variance only when `vo_status!=verified`; never auto-decline on Splunk-only signal.

(6) **Portfolio investors with multiple simultaneous doors** — Small landlords submit parallel apps per unit; benign if underwriting already classifies **`investor`** risk tier. Mitigation: honor **`occupancy=intent_primary`** filters and separate investor taxonomy with higher `apps` ceiling.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud 9.2+ with rights to `index=lending` (or equivalent) containing **Loan Origination System (LOS)** application snapshots or events with `sourcetype=los:application` (name may be customized). Supported commercial LOS families include **Encompass**, **Calyx**, **BytePro**—ingest via **Splunk DB Connect** ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) JDBC to the LOS SQL schema, or vendor **API → HEC** micro-batcher if API is the system of record.
• Privacy and legal: **SSN or national ID** must appear only as **`ssn_hash`** (salted HMAC or tokenization vault id); **property_address** normalized (USPS or country postal validation); **stated_income** units documented (annual vs monthly) and harmonized before variance math; **loan_officer_id** for coaching workflows, not public dashboards.
• Key fields present: `applicant_id`, `ssn_hash`, `property_address`, `stated_income`, `loan_amount`, `application_date`, `loan_officer_id`. Volume planning: **~500–10K applications/month** by institution size—tune `span` and summary jobs accordingly.
• Fraud operations playbooks aligned to **FinCEN** mortgage fraud red-flag narratives and **FFIEC BSA/AML** real-estate guidance; Model Risk or Fair Lending review if automated decisions could impact protected classes—use this search as **prioritization**, not sole decline.
• Reference lookups: **legitimate investor entities** allow list, **relocation program** codes, **new construction** project ids linking multiple units.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**DB Connect pattern**: create input querying `applications` fact with joins to `applicants` and `properties`. Rising column on `last_modified` or `application_id` sequence. Schedule **15–30 minute** intervals for operational fraud; nightly acceptable for smaller shops. Land `index=lending`, `sourcetype=los:application`.

`props.conf`:
```
[los:application]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
EVAL-application_date = strptime(application_date_str, "%Y-%m-%d")
```
If income arrives as strings with currency symbols, strip in **INGEST_EVAL** or **`eval`** macro `clean_money`.

**Verification SPL**:
```
index=lending sourcetype=los:application earliest=-30d
| stats count,
    dc(applicant_id) as applicants,
    dc(loan_officer_id) as officers,
    sum(if(isnotnull(stated_income),1,0)) as with_income
```
Validate **no raw SSN** columns in sample `| fields *`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
```spl
index=lending sourcetype=los:application
| bin _time span=30d
| stats count as apps,
    dc(property_address) as properties,
    dc(ssn_hash) as identities,
    values(stated_income) as incomes
  by applicant_id, _time
| where apps > 3 OR properties > 2
| eval income_variance=if(mvcount(incomes)>1, max(incomes)-min(incomes), 0)
| where income_variance > 20000 OR apps > 3
| sort -apps
```

Understanding this SPL
`| bin _time span=30d` creates **rolling window buckets** on event time—ensure application_date aligns with `_time` assignment at ingest; otherwise use **`| bin application_date span=30d`** after `eval` conversion. **`stats` by `applicant_id`** collapses multiple applications per borrower in each window, counting velocity (`apps`), footprint across properties, and whether **hashed identities** suggest merged profiles (should be 1—higher may indicate data quality issues or synthetic splits). **`values(stated_income)`** collects multivalued incomes across applications; **`income_variance`** is a blunt instrument—confirm **annualized normalization** before trusting the `20000` threshold (tune regionally). The logic **OR**s high velocity with property hopping and income inconsistency—analysts should treat this as **triage**, then pull **VOE** status, **ATR** calculations, and **appraisal** variance from auxiliary indexes.

Schedule as Alert: **weekly** `0 8 * * 1`, range **`-60d` to now**, trigger **number of results > 0**, **throttle** per `applicant_id` **30d** after first case opened. For high-volume retail, **daily** digest email instead of paging.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) **Unit harmonization**: confirm `stated_income` all in **annual USD**; if not, apply `| eval stated_income=case(...)` with documented conversion.
(b) **Identity logic**: `dc(ssn_hash)` should usually be 1; if >1, fix hash pipeline before interpreting fraud.
(c) **Address normalization**: run dedup test on `property_address` after postal standardization (`| eval prop_key=md5(upper(trim(property_address)))`).
(d) **Backtest**: known **straw buyer** ring from law-enforcement referral should elevate in pilot.
(e) **Fair lending sampling**: disproportionate flagged demographics triggers manual policy review—not a SPL change but a governance gate.
(f) **Officer clustering**: supplementary search `stats count by loan_officer_id` reveals **broker coaching** opportunities vs systemic fraud.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: **four panels**—**(1)** bubble/scatter **`apps` vs `properties`** sized by **`income_variance`**; **(2)** choropleth or clustered map layer on **property** coordinates (only if geo permitted); **(3)** investigator table listing `applicant_id`, **`apps`, `properties`, `income_variance`, `loan_officer_id`, last status** from CRM lookup join; **(4)** KPI single-value **applications flagged / 1000 booked** trailing 90d**.**

Alerting: **Case management** (Actimize-style) webhook; **Teams** `#fraud-mortgage`; **no auto-decline** from Splunk alert alone.

Runbook:
1. Analyst validates **Relocation**/**Builder** allowance tags.
2. Pull **credit** inquires timeline; correlate with **`apps`** cadence.
3. Order enhanced **VOE / VOD** when income variance unexplained.
4. Escalate to **Special Investigations** if property flips correlate with **`loan_officer`** concentration.
5. Log outcome; adjust **`20000`** threshold via change control quarterly.
6. Privacy: scrub exports of **`property_address`** to last-known city if sharing externally.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Explosion of identities** — Hash salt rotated mid-window; stabilize salt versioning.

• **Huge income variance benign** — **Bonus season** vs base conflation; ingest `income_components`.

• **`apps` count inflated** — **Pre-approval soft pulls** vs formal apps; filter `stage=in_submission`.

• **Stale `_time`** — DB Connect uses batch load time; remap to **`application_date`** for `bin`.

• **Null addresses** — New construction placeholders; join **project master** lookup.

• **Fair lending alarm** — Disparate impact in flagged cohort—pause automated routing, keep manual review.


Loan product taxonomy, occupancy intent, channel codes, and AUS waterfall context
Operationalizing LOS velocity alerts requires narrating whether applications are retail **purchase**, **refi**, **HELOC**, **construction-to-perm**, **non-QM**, **portfolio**, or **correspondent** channel—each legitimately inflates `apps` or `properties` without fraud. Enrich Splunk rows at ingest with `product_type`, `occupancy_intent`, `channel`, `aus_engine`, `aus_decision`, `appraisal_type`, `preapproval_flag`, and `conditional_status` so analysts filter noise before escalating. Automated Underwriting Systems (DU/LPA or proprietary) downgrade/income-documentation tiers change `stated_income` deltas benignly—join `aus_doc_class` lookups so variance spikes tied to borrower-provided wage vs bank-statement programs do not falsely imply document forgery alone.

Income normalization, amortization quirks, commission vs W-2, and tipped wages
Mortgage underwriting frequently toggles among **annualized base**, **prior-year averages**, **YTD paystub extrapolation**, and **bonus seasonality**. Document deterministic transforms in **`macros/clean_stated_income`** so SPL variance math references apples-to-apples units. Gig-economy, restaurant tipped income, seasonal teachers, and union strike/return events create believable large swings—pair Splunk hits with payroll vendor tokens or third-party **VOE** provider status fields. Self-employed borrowers often restate income after CPA review; treat first-pass vs final `stated_income` as separate fields (`stated_income_initial`, `stated_income_final`) to avoid punishing healthy revision cycles.

Property address hygiene, geocoding, and new construction master plans
Cluster maps become trustworthy only when addresses pass **USPS CASS** / international postal validation; maintain `property_key` separate from free-text `property_address` to collapse apartment vs unit formatting dupes. New construction projects sometimes share a **sales trailer** address early then split into lot-specific addresses—use builder `community_id` enrichment to avoid false multi-property churn. **PUD** / **condo** assignments can mis-geo if only mailing addresses flow from LOS—join assessor parcel ids when legally permitted.

Co-borrowers, non-occupant co-borrowers, trusts, and LLC shells
Application velocity may reflect **co-borrower addition / removal** cycles across related files with distinct `applicant_id` keys but shared `ssn_hash` families—where legal, maintain `household_id` tokenization distinct from individual applicants to reduce false ring alerts. **Trust and LLC** shells require commercial risk tiering; mortgage retail thresholds should auto-downgrade when `entity_type` indicates business purpose.

Appraisal, AMC, AVM, and valuation fraud adjunct signals
Velocity alone rarely proves fraud; operationalize optional joins to **appraisal** scores, **AVM** spreads, **AMC** vendor ids, revision counts, and **CU** (Collateral Underwriter) flags as secondary panels—without changing the core `spl`. Document fair lending review when geography overlays might proxy protected classes; restrict map access to specialized roles.

Credit pull timing, rate locks, LO comp plans, and ethical walls
Correlate application bursts with **hard pull** timestamps and **lock desk** policies; promotional **float-down** weeks create benign parallel applications. **Loan officer compensation** plans that reward speed can accidentally cluster marginal applications—HR business partners may request anonymized LO cohort stats; deliver aggregated metrics only with privacy counsel sign-off.

HMDA / fair lending monitoring alignment
When Splunk exports feed **HMDA** disparity reviews, ensure fields used (race/ethnicity proxies must NOT be invented) align with regulatory definitions; this UC intentionally omits protected attributes from detection SPL—use only for post-hoc sampling stratification under legal guidance.

Identity theft vs first-party fraud differentiation
High `apps` with stable `stated_income` but suspicious **device fingerprint** or **IP geolocation** jumps may indicate third-party identity theft rather than straw buying—integrate optional `device_id` / `session_risk` indexes with strict retention if available.

Document intelligence and eClose telemetry
**eNote**, **Remote Online Notarization** events, **eVault** timestamps, and **closing disclosure** version counts help explain velocity when borrowers rapidly iterate documents—ingest as optional sourcetypes for narrative context.

Servicing handoff and loan sale data breaks
Sometimes duplicate application rows appear when loans **trade** to new servicers and snapshots re-ingest—dedupe on `loan_file_id` + `snapshot_version` metadata.

Operational runbook expansions beyond core steps
Maintain **scenario library** bridging FinCEN/FFIEC red flags (employment/income inconsistencies, unexplained deposits, inflated appraisals, straw buyer linkage) mapped to SPL drilldown starters analysts can fork. Quarterly rotate scenarios through tabletop exercises referencing Splunk timelines.

Mortgage insurer and investor overlays (FHA, VA, USDA, PMI)
Investors may trigger multiple submissions per borrower correcting **MIC** / certificate numbers—populate `submission_attempt` counters separate from substantive `apps` when vendors permit.

Cyber overlays (credential stuffing on borrower portals)
Burst LOS activity may originate from scripted **portal** scraping rather than insider fraud—join WAF/`http_event` summaries when LOS vendor exposes API auditing.

Portfolio retention analytics
Retail banks refinancing their own portfolios may inflate apparent velocity—suppress when **`prior_servicer`** equals in-house sentinel.

Mortgage LOS DB Connect schema pitfalls
Wide tables with redundant joins can duplicate applicant rows inflating counts—enforce `dedup applicant_id,file_version` in DB Connect SQL or use `summary` transforms. Handle **soft-deleted** rows via `is_active` flags.

Validation deepening (g–l)
(g) **Stage-gate alignment**: ensure only `submitted` or `underwriting` stages count toward `apps` after business signs off. (h) **Geo consent**: confirm map panels respect state privacy opt-outs. (i) **Kids** & **Power of Attorney** scenarios documented for manual review. (j) **Military SCRA** protections context for payment-change churn. (k) **Disaster forbearance** programs inflating temporary applications. (l) **Portfolio acquisition** bulk file imports tagged `bulk_import=true` for suppression windows.

Troubleshooting expansions
• **Hash collisions on `ssn_hash`** — Rare but catastrophic; rotate salt and reindex under change control.
• **Trailing whitespace on addresses** — Inflate `dc(property_address)`; normalize with `trim`.
• **Currency vs USD** — international applicants with dual currency documents skew income variance; harmonize FX.
• **Duplicate `_time` from batch jobs** — distorts `bin span=30d`; pin to `application_date`.
• **Co-applicant double counting** — each applicant_id row duplicates property; pivot to `loan_file_id` when available.
• **Marketing lead gen vs apps** — pre-qual leads should not enter same sourcetype without stage tag.

Case management integration details
Map Splunk alert JSON to **Actimize**, **SAS**, **FICO**, or ServiceNow fields: priority, queue, analyst skill tag, required documents checklist, Reg E vs mortgage fraud distinction, narrative boilerplates.

Vendor API / HEC micro-batching operational notes
When LOS vendor enforces **rate limits**, implement exponential backoff in HEC forwarder scripts; log `429` responses to `index=ops` for SRE visibility.

Executive reporting pack
Monthly PDF summarizing flagged count, overturn rate after human review, average loss avoided estimates (if modeled), borrower impact (applications delayed median minutes).

Continuous improvement loop
Retro-label Splunk-flagged cohorts quarterly with adjudicated outcomes feeding precision/recall dashboard for model governance—even if Splunk SPL remains symbolic rules.

Legal subpoena responsiveness
Maintain runbook bridging Splunk **`collect`** jobs extracting minimal necessary columns for subpoenas referencing mortgage fraud investigations with DPO approvals.

Climate relocation and disaster displacement nuance
Hurricane / wildfire relocations inflate multi-state applications legitimately—ingest **`fema_disaster_declaration`** lookups for contextual overlays similar to relocation programs already mitigated elsewhere in this UC.

Student loan refinancing cross-sell noise
Retail banks pushing student refi concurrently can duplicate marketing touches causing multiple apps—coordinate with **`campaign_id`** metadata.

Blockchain / crypto down-payment sourcing
Emerging underwriting checks referencing crypto liquidation may cause income narrative volatility—finance SMEs annotate benign patterns in lookup form.

Investor delivery, MERS, and trailing doc exceptions
Velocity may reflect **trailing document** resubmissions correcting **MERS** MIN issues or investor **stip** cures—tag `post_close_doc_wave` so Splunk does not treat each upload as a distinct application without business agreement.

Warehouse line capacity and rate sheet volatility
Mortgage banking operations sometimes encourage rapid refi waves when **warehouse** capacity or **hedge** desks signal windows—link optional `rate_sheet_version` fields to explain cohort surges.

Warehouse fraud and early payment default cross-signals
Partner with **EDD** teams monitoring **EPD** spikes 60–120 days post-close; funnel Splunk hits into early default watchlists with legal boundaries.

Non-arm’s-length transactions and family sales
Family sales and **non-arm’s-length** purchases often cluster addresses and amounts—maintain `relationship_flag` from LOS free text mining (governed) before auto-escalating.

Appraisal waiver / PIW programs
**Property inspection waivers** inflate velocity when borrowers shop multiple properties rapidly with light touch—contextualize versus full appraisal paths.

Government program churn (FHA streamline, VA IRRRL)
Program-specific marketing creates synchronized multi-app waves—join `product_program` tags for seasonal suppress macros.

Second home vs investment misclassification
Mis-tagged occupancy can cause property-count inflation when borrowers convert intent—use `occupancy_verification_status` from underwriting.

Non-US regulatory overlays (UK AML, Canada FINTRAC)
International subsidiaries must map Splunk alerts to local STR/SAR analogs and translation requirements for narrative fields.

Document retention and GLBA
Train analysts that exported drilldown CSVs are **GLBA**-sensitive even when SSN hashed—use encrypted shares only.

Redlining / fair lending sentinel tests
Run periodic **disparate impact** reviews on flagged vs booked populations with legal sampling plans; do not embed protected class into detection SPL.

Cyber account takeover on borrower accounts
If LOS vendor supports **MFA** telemetry, optional join can distinguish synthetic application storms from human rings.

Machine learning augmentation boundaries
If org adds **gradient boosting** risk scores to dashboard, keep this rules-based UC as interpretable baseline referenced in model risk files.

Capacity planning for retail refi booms
Elastic auto-scaling guidance for DB Connect workers pulling wide LOS rows; watch JDBC connection pool exhaustion during Monday morning spikes.

Cross-sell with retail deposit fraud
Optional cross-index to `index=retail` **`zelle`** or **ACH** fraud only when MOU covers purpose limitation.

Final go-live checklist excerpt
Sign-off from Chief Risk Officer designee, Fraud Operations lead, Fair Lending officer, Privacy counsel, LOS vendor success manager, Splunk platform owner, and IAM team for role-based field masks.

Quarterly threshold governance
Threshold `20000` variance and `apps>3` revisit with written agenda: precision, recall, overturn analysis, economic environment commentary, marketing campaign calendar, rate environment.

Training for underwriters as Splunk-lite users
Teach reading panel tooltips, interpreting `income_variance` under documentation paths, understanding false positive mitigations from relocation and builder programs documented in this UC’s `knownFalsePositives` companion field.

Long-term monitoring roadmap
Year-one focuses on precision tuning; year-two adds orthogonal **phone number** / **email hash** velocity; year-three explores **title** / **escrow** timeline correlation if title partners share limited metadata under contract.

Escalation paths for law enforcement referrals
When SAR narratives cite Splunk outputs, store analyst notes referencing **FinCEN** guidance on technical indicator phrasing without over-claiming deterministic proof.

Audit trail of lookup mutations
Version `relocation_program.csv` and `investor_allowlist.csv` in Git with approvals; Splunk `lookup` refresh should log `git_sha` into a metadata index for examiners.

Post-close fraud monitoring handoff
When loans move to **`index=servicing`**, recycle velocity concepts carefully—privacy bases shift; reuse only hashed keys approved for servicing analytics.

Climate data center / resiliency overlaps
LOS vendor SaaS outages causing application timestamp clumping should not be mistaken for fraud bursts—ingest vendor status RSS or health-check events for correlation.

Controlled vocabulary for escalation narratives
Standardize verbs in ticketing (“velocity anomaly,” “income discrepancy,” “geographic anomaly”) aligning with FFIEC glossary expectations so downstream QA of SAR drafting stays consistent across analysts.

Quarterly fair lending joint review minutes
Store meeting notes referencing Splunk KPIs with legal counsel attendance for defensible governance.
grandmaExplanation: When someone churns out too many home-loan paperwork packets for different houses in just a month, or the paycheck numbers do not tell a steady story across those packets, we raise a hand before the bank inks bad paper.
